issue.title: Non-code files pollute graph as code symbols
issue.body:
## Steps to reproduce
1. Index any repo that contains markdown, XML, or config files
2. Call `query(query="project configuration", repo="...")` or `detect_changes()`

## Actual behavior
CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, README.md, pom.xml, pyproject.toml appear as graph nodes and in query results. `detect_changes` treats them as code symbols.

## Expected behavior
Non-code files (markdown, XML configs, TOML configs) should either be excluded from the graph or clearly categorized as documentation/config nodes, not mixed with code symbols.

## User impact
Query results and change detection are polluted with irrelevant file entries, degrading signal-to-noise ratio across all tools.

## Evidence
```json
query(query="project configuration") →
{ "definitions": [{ "name": "CLAUDE.md" }, { "name": "README.md" }, ...] }

detect_changes() → includes pom.xml and pyproject.toml as "changed symbols"
```
